rulings.Case Title: ANDREW VAIL VS. GARLOCK SEALING TECHNOLOGIES, LLC, FKA GARLOCK, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO AMEND Complaint As To Novelis Corporation Only
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 1. PLAINTIFF ANDREW VAIL'S MOTION TO AMEND Complaint As To Novelis Corporation Only IS GRANTED. ALTHOUGH THERE HAS BEEN SOME DELAY, DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT IT WILL BE PREJUDICED BY THE AMENDMENT FOR IT DID QUESTION PLAINTIFF ABOUT THE FACILITY WHERE HE WORKED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Dismiss Action & Arbitration Proceeding For Delay In Prosecution; Request To The Clerk Of 302; Request For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 2. DEFENDANT CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC.'S SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO ELLER MEDIA COMPANY, MOTION To Dismiss Action & Arbitration Proceeding For Delay In Prosecution (Request For Judicial NOTICE) IS DENIED, GIVEN THAT THE PARTIES WILL ARBITRATE IN JULY. =(302/PJM)

DEFENDANT PACIFIC MECHANICAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDARPER MOVING PARTY. =(302/PJM/PB) ****TENTATIVE RULING CORRECTED ON 4-29-08 TO SHOW MATTER IS OFF CALENDAR, PER MOVING PARTY - TENTATIVE RULING WENT OUT AS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED *******

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' et al VS. MATTHEW C KRAMER
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANT MATTHEW KRAMER, STATE OF CALIFORNIA AND DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS AND REHABILITATION'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 30, 2008 PER STIPULATION. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: IRENE MCGEE VS. YOLANDE VITENSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-08-472094 WITH CASE CGC-07-466650
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANTS YOLANDE VITENSON AND KARL LYON'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-08-472094 WITH CASE CGC-07-466650 IS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE CASES ARE CONSOLIDATED FOR ALL PURPOSES AND CASE 466650 IS DESIGNATED AS THE LEAD CASE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. LEO MCFADDEN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANT LEO MCFADDEN'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL OPPORTUNITY TO AMEND. IN THE PROPOSED THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF MUST SET FORTH THE DATES WHEN DEFENDANT ALLEGEDLY THREATENED "TO HAVE PLAINTIFF KILLED IN AT LEAST TWO OCCASIONS DURING TELEPHONE CALLS IN WHICH DEFENDANT COMPLAINED TO PLAINTIFF ABOUT PLAINTIFF'S LAWFUL PETITIONING." THE AMENDMENT IS TO BE FILED WITHIN TEN DAYS OF COURT'S ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: VIGNOBLES JEAN BOIREAU EARL, A CORPORATION VS. PIVOTAL, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANT PIVOTAL, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF HAD THE RIGHT TO SERVE DEFENDANT'S AGENT BY LEAVING THE SUMMONS WITH AN ADULT WHO WAS APPARENTLY IN CHARGE. C.C.P. SECTION 415.20(A). =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAL SASSANO VS. EDUARD SELYUTIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANTS EDUARD SELYUTIN, FELIX PROFIS AND CALROS INTERNATIONAL CO.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED BASED ON THE DECLARATIONS SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANTS. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN BREITENSTEIN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. PLAINTIFF'S ORIGINAL COMPLAINT MAKES CLEAR THAT THE INJURY OCCURRED AFTER PLAINTIFF DISEMBARKED FROM THE LIGHT RAIL VEHICLE. PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT CONTRADICTS THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT IN THAT IT ATTEMPTS TO ALLEGE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS INJURED WHILE DISEMBARKING. SUCH ALLEGATIONS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED. POTTER V ARIZONA SOUTHERN COACH LINES (1988) 202 CAL APP 3D 126, 133 FN 2. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike The First, Second, Third, Eighth, And Ninth Causes Of Action Of Pltf'S Complaint

rulings.Case Title: BERGMANGAME, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. STEPHEN J. BOWKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner Or In The Alternative For An Order To Post Bond By Date Certain; Request For Attorney Fees; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 11. PLAINTIFF STEPHEN BOWKER'S Motion To Dismiss Appeal Of The Order, Decision Or Award Of The Labor Commissioner Or In The Alternative For An Order To Post Bond By Date Certain; Request For Attorney Fees; Request For Judicial Notice IS CONTINUED TO MAY 12, 2008, TO GIVE DEFENDANT A FINAL OPPORTUNITY TO OBTAIN A BOND. IF A BOND HAS NOT BEEN OBTAINED BY THAT DATE, THE APPEAL SHALL BE DISMISSED ON MAY 13TH. PROGRESSIVE CONCRETE, INC., V PARKER (2006) 136 CAL. APP. 4TH 540. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GAETANO ZANELLI VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 12. PETITIONER GAETANO ZANELLI'S MOTION To Tax Costs IS DENIED. THE MOVING PARTY HAS FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT THE COSTS WERE UNNECESSARY OR UNREASONABLE. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH KAPLAN VS. NAJIB YASER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 13. PLAINTIFF JOSEPH KAPLAN'S Motion For Summary Judgment IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: TROPHY PROPERTIES VIII,LLC VS. LEWIS BENNETT et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, APRIL 29, 2008 LINE 14. DEFENDANT LEWIS BENNETT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, FIVE DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF MAY PLEAD ALTERNATIVE THEORIES OF LIABILILTY AND ISSUES OF FACT MAY NOT BE RESOLVED BY DEMURRER. =(302/PJM/PB)
